title: Tococa symphyandra
intro: Tococa symphyandra, commonly known as the "Mangrove Tococa," is a species of flowering plant in the family Tococaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Tococa symphyandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as tococa alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tococa symphyandra is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, which is used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, fever, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Tococa symphyandra is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It is native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. Tococa symphyandra is a member of the genus Tococa, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The plant is characterized by its unique flowers that are shaped like a pitcher, attracting and collecting nectar from insects. Tococa symphyandra is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Ochnaceae family.
